title: viola cornuta subsp. montcaunica
intro: Viola cornuta subsp. montcaunica, also known as the mountain pansy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the violet family (Violaceae). It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, particularly in its roots, leaves, and flowers, which contain flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic activities. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as digestive issues, like diarrhea and gastritis. Herbal preparations of Viola cornuta subsp. montcaunica include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used internally or applied topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
